pattern: Halving a bigger number always removes more from the total than halving a smaller one, so greedily always halving the current largest value (via a max-heap) reaches the target reduction in the fewest steps.
hints: Halving the same absolute amount matters less than halving the biggest available number, since halving a large value removes the largest possible chunk right now.
After you halve a number, it can be halved again later - so the set of candidates to pick from keeps changing, which is exactly what a max-heap is for.

Push every value onto a max-heap; repeatedly pop the current largest, add half of it to your running reduction, push the halved value back, and count the operation, stopping as soon as the total reduction reaches at least half the original sum.

edge_inputs.description: typical mixed values needing several rounds
one large value dominates the sum
single element, one halving always reduces it by at least half of itself
all equal values, ties in the heap must still pick correctly each round
odd values whose halves are fractional and must accumulate as floats, not truncate
large uniform values near the constraint ceiling

stress.description: 10000 random values across the full magnitude range, exercising the max-heap under load
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 10000001) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: no special structure required; correctness of the greedy holds for any positive values

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def halveArray(self, nums):
        values = list(map(float, nums))
        target = sum(values) / 2.0
        reduced = 0.0
        ops = 0
        while reduced < target - 1e-9:
            idx = max(range(len(values)), key=lambda i: values[i])
            half = values[idx] / 2.0
            reduced += half
            values[idx] = half
            ops += 1
        return ops

solutions.python:
import heapq

class Solution:
    def halveArray(self, nums):
        heap = [-float(x) for x in nums]
        heapq.heapify(heap)
        total = -sum(heap)
        target = total / 2.0
        reduced = 0.0
        ops = 0
        while reduced < target - 1e-9:
            largest = -heapq.heappop(heap)
            half = largest / 2.0
            reduced += half
            heapq.heappush(heap, -half)
            ops += 1
        return ops

complexity.time: O(n log n + ops log n)
